body: Outline is set. Three vignettes, framework anchors the assessment chapter. Drafting from here.
Moving to Done. The structure finally holds together without forcing it.
Vignettes are the hard part. Keeping names and details fully fictional, no patient anywhere near this.
Halfway through the draft. The middle section still reads stiff. Will loosen it on the next pass.
Citations cleaned for chapters 1-3. Two references had no DOI; tracked down the originals.
All APA-formatted and verified. Ready for the editor read. Closing.
Folding the definitions into the text reads better than a separate glossary. Dropping this for the current edition.
Nadine sent the committee revisions. The case write-up weighting moves from 20 to 30 percent. Reworking the rubric to match.
Rubric draft done. Need to confirm the rounding rule with Nadine before this goes in the syllabus.
Landlord still owes me the CAM reconciliation figures. Cannot sign the renewal until those are in writing. Chasing this week.
Reworked the middle section. It reads cleaner now and the second vignette lands the point about deference to elders in the assessment. Better.
Pulled the two newer review articles for the spring module. Still deciding whether to keep the older guideline piece for historical context or just cut it.
Cut the 2018 piece. The current treatment guidelines cover it better. Reading list is down to twelve required, four optional.
Nadine confirmed: round at the module level, not per assignment. Rubric is final. Folding it into the syllabus now.
CAM figures finally arrived. The 2025 reconciliation came in $1,400 over the estimate. Asking the landlord to itemize before I accept it.
Itemization checked out. Two HVAC line items I had questioned were legitimate. Comfortable moving the renewal forward now.
Holding on this until the lease is actually signed. No point sending Anthony a draft schedule that changes if the terms move.
Going back to the outline to add a fourth vignette anchor in chapter five. The book needs one example from an immigrant elder context to round out the set.
Editor flagged three citations in chapter two that need page numbers added. Quick fix, doing it now.
Loaded the final reading list into the Nova course shell. Spring section is ready on the readings side. Rubric next.
Full draft of the assessment chapter is done. Letting it sit a week before the revision pass so I read it with fresh eyes.
Sample case write-up scored against the new rubric to sanity check the weighting. The 30 percent feels right - it rewards the formulation work without burying the rest.
